reasons: Lolita can reduce Moskov's damage window by blocking and reflecting ranged basic attacks and projectile pressure.
She protects teammates when Moskov tries to free-hit from a fixed angle.

proof.summary: Moskov depends heavily on repeated ranged basic attacks, while Lolita's shield can reflect incoming ranged basic attacks and projectiles during the key DPS window.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Moskov before he commits his attack-speed window.
Teammates stay behind the shield instead of splitting randomly.
failureCases: Moskov flanks from the side or waits until Lolita's shield is unavailable.

reasons: Belerick punishes Moskov's repeated basic attacks with return-style damage pressure.
His taunt can interrupt Moskov's free-hit pattern and force Moskov to attack a tank instead of the backline.

proof.summary: Moskov wants to stack continuous basic attacks, while Belerick rewards being hit and can taunt Moskov during the fight.
worksBestWhen: Belerick is positioned between Moskov and the backline.
Belerick has enough durability to survive the first DPS window.
failureCases: Moskov avoids hitting Belerick and attacks from another angle.
Belerick dives too far without follow-up.

reasons: Natalia can threaten Moskov before he gets safe positioning.
Smoke Bomb gives Natalia a strong answer against basic-attack marksmen during assassination or disengage windows.

proof.summary: Moskov is dangerous when protected and free-hitting, while Natalia can approach from unseen angles and use Smoke Bomb to reduce basic-attack retaliation.
worksBestWhen: Natalia tracks Moskov's lane rotation and attacks before teamfight formation.
Moskov has already used Abyss Walker.
failureCases: Moskov stays grouped with peel heroes.
Natalia enters after Moskov has vision and a wall-stun angle prepared.

reasons: Saber can punish Moskov's low-durability marksman profile with targeted airborne burst.
This is strongest after Moskov uses his blink or is separated from peel.

proof.summary: Moskov wants space to attack repeatedly, while Saber can force a single-target burst check before Moskov can sustain DPS.
worksBestWhen: Saber waits for Moskov's blink to be used.
Saber has enough burst damage to finish or force Moskov out.
failureCases: Moskov has Purify, defensive item timing, or strong peel.
Saber dives into multiple enemies without follow-up.

reasons: Franco can punish Moskov if he is locked before he starts free-hitting.
Suppression is valuable because it prevents Moskov from using his mobility or basic-attack window while controlled.

proof.summary: Moskov's threat depends on maintaining attack range, while Franco can suppress him and create a pickoff window.
worksBestWhen: Franco hides hook or Flicker-ultimate angles.
failureCases: Franco misses the engage or catches the wrong target.
Moskov stays behind tanks and avoids hook angles.
